items.original_text: Null hypothesis wins again. Crémieux: We now have a within-person study of the effects of GLP-1 usage on divorce, employment, psychological distress, self-rated health, and depression. They don't seem to have positive or negative effects!
items.zh_text: 零假设再次获胜。Crémieux：我们现在有一项关于使用GLP-1对离婚、就业、心理困扰、自评健康和抑郁影响的个体内研究。它们似乎没有正面或负面影响！

items.original_text: “Suicidal empathy” Molly Crane-Newman: “I’m saying fuck Brian Thompson. I don’t give a flying fuck he died,” says Ashley Rojas, wearing her press badge provided by @NYCMayorsOffice. Lena Weissbrot adds that Thompson’s teenage sons “are better off without him” and should “enjoy the blood money.”
items.zh_text: “自杀式共情” 莫莉·克兰-纽曼报道：阿什莉·罗哈斯佩戴着@NYCMayorsOffice提供的记者证说：“我他妈的说布莱恩·汤普森活该。我他妈的根本不在乎他死了。”莉娜·韦斯布罗特补充说，汤普森十几岁的儿子们“没有他过得更好”，并且应该“享受那笔血钱”。

items.original_text: Mauv’s Law: Perceived hazards grow to absorb whatever margin is available to mitigate them, many such cases. mauv: is there a name for this specific parkinson's law type thing, where perceived hazards grow to absorb whatever margin is available to mitigate them
items.zh_text: 莫夫定律：感知到的危险会膨胀，以吸收任何可用于缓解它们的余量，此类案例比比皆是。莫夫：这种特定的帕金森定律式现象有没有一个名称，即感知到的危险会膨胀，以吸收任何可用于缓解它们的余量

items.original_text: There is no doubt Gemini will max benchmarks - they always do The only question is whether it’s actually a good model or will it simply be a benchmaxxed model
items.zh_text: 毫无疑问，Gemini 会在基准测试中取得最高分——他们总是如此。唯一的问题是，它到底是一个好模型，还是仅仅是一个刷榜模型。

items.original_text: All Firewall mitigations are now fully free on @vercel. Not just DDoS and system-level mitigations, but also any rule you configure. Vercel now absorbs the computational and network costs of any size of attack or traffic mitigation for your peace of mind. (And more to come!) Vercel Developers: All firewall-mitigated traffic is now free on Vercel. Starting today, you aren't charged for requests that are denied, challenged, or rate-limited by Vercel Firewall. This extends free DDoS mitigation to rules you configure. https://vercel.com/changelog/web-application-firewall-mitigated-traffic-is-free-on-vercel
items.zh_text: 所有防火墙缓解措施现已在 @vercel 上完全免费。不仅是 DDoS 和系统级缓解措施，还包括您配置的任何规则。Vercel 现在承担任何规模攻击或流量缓解的计算和网络成本，让您安心。（还有更多功能即将推出！）Vercel 开发者：所有由防火墙缓解的流量现已在 Vercel 上免费。从今天开始，您无需为被 Vercel 防火墙拒绝、质询或限流的请求付费。这还将免费 DDoS 缓解扩展到了您配置的规则。https://vercel.com/changelog/web-application-firewall-mitigated-traffic-is-free-on-vercel

items.original_text: Keep your Mac awake so you can build and work from your phone, with Codex in the ChatGPT app: OpenAI Developers: Your Mac can hold down the fort while you work from your phone. Enable remote connection in the Codex desktop app, then turn on “Keep this Mac awake.” When your Mac is powered on and plugged in, Codex can keep running there while you work from the ChatGPT mobile app.
items.zh_text: 让您的 Mac 保持唤醒状态，这样您就可以通过 ChatGPT 应用中的 Codex 在手机上构建和工作：OpenAI 开发者：您的 Mac 可以在您通过手机工作时坚守岗位。在 Codex 桌面应用中启用远程连接，然后打开“保持此 Mac 唤醒”。当您的 Mac 开机并接通电源时，Codex 可以继续在那里运行，而您则通过 ChatGPT 移动应用进行工作。

items.original_text: how to use /goal in codex — keep Codex working on a persistent objective until it's solved: Derrick Choi: My colleagues wrote up a great post on using Goals in Codex. They go through when to use them, what changes when a Goal is active, and how to write Goals that give Codex a clear outcome, constraints and verification criteria. Also how we designed Goals at the architecture level
items.zh_text: 如何在 Codex 中使用 /goal —— 让 Codex 持续朝着一个固定目标工作，直到问题解决：Derrick Choi：我的同事们写了一篇关于在 Codex 中使用 Goal 的精彩文章。他们介绍了何时使用 Goal、Goal 激活后会发生什么变化，以及如何编写能向 Codex 提供明确结果、约束条件和验证标准的 Goal。还介绍了我们如何在架构层面设计 Goal。

items.original_text: Codex for unsubscribing from unwanted marketing emails Todd Saunders: I finally used /goal in Codex and I’m absolutely mind blown. I had it look through my last 500 archived emails. Then look for an unsubscribe link and unsubscribe if there is one. It found 87 and it clicked them all. It handled the “are you sure” pages AND flagged 14 that
items.zh_text: Greg Brockman：用于取消订阅不需要的营销邮件的 Codex。Todd Saunders：我终于在 Codex 中使用了 /goal，这让我彻底震惊。我让它浏览了我最近 500 封存档的邮件，然后寻找取消订阅链接，如果有的话就取消订阅。它找到了 87 封，并点击了所有链接。它还处理了“你确定吗”页面，并标记了 14 封。

items.original_text: RT Daniel Jeffries Finally a semi-useful read on Mythos that is free of myth and talks about what this means more practically (not this is the end of the world as we know it, but how do we deal with faster patches and attacks from AI as other models scale to chained exploits)? This is the kind of conversation we need, not idiotic ones about the end of all software. We need "what is the right answer?" because these models are coming and will get better so how to we put our heads together and make better/more secure software across the world? And it can't just be patching the 100 or so projects that got access to Project Glasswing. That is not gonna help the world. We need to figure out how does everyone else who is not part of the special chosen people to get blessed with access to test and patch their stuff, aka the open source projects and closed software that is not Office or Cloudflare but the 99.99% of software that runs everything else in the world? What is the right loop cycle to help people patch and fix things at the source? In the long run, AI will make software more secure, not less. But it will change how teams have to work to get there. Figuring that out means putting it in more team's hands sooner rather than later. Cloudflare: Cloudflare's security team spent the last few weeks testing Anthropic's Mythos against fifty of our own repositories. What we learned about offensive AI, why faster patching is the wrong reaction, and what the architecture around vulnerabilities has to look like next.
items.zh_text: Yann LeCun 转发 Daniel Jeffries：终于有一篇关于 Mythos 的半实用读物，它没有神话色彩，而是更实际地讨论了这意味着什么（不是“世界末日来了”，而是“当其他模型规模扩大到链式攻击时，我们如何应对更快的补丁和来自 AI 的攻击”？）。这才是我们需要的对话，而不是那些关于“所有软件终结”的愚蠢言论。我们需要问“正确答案是什么？”，因为这些模型正在到来，并且会变得更好，所以我们如何集思广益，在全球范围内制造更好/更安全的软件？而且这不能仅仅修补那大约 100 个获得了 Project Glasswing 访问权限的项目。那对世界没有帮助。我们需要弄清楚，那些不属于被特别选中、有幸获得访问权限来测试和修补自己东西的其他人——也就是开源项目，以及不是 Office 或 Cloudflare 但运行着世界上其他一切的 99.99% 的闭源软件——该怎么办？什么是正确的循环周期，来帮助人们从源头修补和修复问题？长远来看，AI 会让软件更安全，而不是更不安全。但它会改变团队达成这一目标的工作方式。弄清楚这一点意味着要尽早让更多团队参与进来。Cloudflare：Cloudflare 的安全团队在过去几周里，针对我们自己的 50 个代码仓库测试了 Anthropic 的 Mythos。我们学到了关于进攻性 AI 的知识，为什么更快的补丁是错误的反应，以及围绕漏洞的架构接下来应该是什么样子。

items.original_text: RT Andrew Weinstein When a federal agency tasked with protecting all Americans decides to mandate a specific religious worldview, it ceases to protect democracy and begins to threaten it.
items.zh_text: 转推 Andrew Weinstein：当一个负责保护所有美国人的联邦机构决定强制推行特定的宗教世界观时，它就不再保护民主，而是开始威胁民主。

items.original_text: RT nic carter Re This is the single most important chart. If AI were driving prices, you'd see a cluster top-right. You don't. States with huge load growth (VA, TX, NV, ND, IA) sit at ~0c change in 5y. States with massive price hikes (CA, NY, MA, CT) have basically NO load growth.
items.zh_text: 转发 @nic carter：这是唯一最重要的图表。如果人工智能在推动价格上涨，你会看到右上角有一个密集区域。但事实并非如此。负荷增长巨大的州（弗吉尼亚州、得克萨斯州、内华达州、北达科他州、爱荷华州）5年内电价变化约为0美分。而电价大幅上涨的州（加利福尼亚州、纽约州、马萨诸塞州、康涅狄格州）基本上没有负荷增长。

items.original_text: A 𝚏𝚒𝚛𝚎𝚠𝚊𝚕𝚕 your agents will love. One of the coolest things about Vercel’s Firewall is how hard the team worked on instant global propagation (~300ms). Imagine if 𝚒𝚙𝚝𝚊𝚋𝚕𝚎𝚜 took *minutes* to propagate? That’s the average industry CDN/WAF experience! Vercel Developers: The new Vercel CLI 𝚏𝚒𝚛𝚎𝚠𝚊𝚕𝚕 command brings firewall configuration to the terminal. You and your agents can now manage custom rules, IP blocks, system mitigations, Attack Mode, and more: https://vercel.com/changelog/manage-vercel-firewall-in-the-cli
items.zh_text: 一个你的智能体们会爱上的防火墙。Vercel 防火墙最酷的一点是团队在即时全球传播（约300毫秒）上付出的巨大努力。想象一下如果 iptables 需要*几分钟*才能传播？这就是行业平均 CDN/WAF 的体验！Vercel 开发者：新的 Vercel CLI firewall 命令将防火墙配置带到了终端。你和你的智能体现在可以管理自定义规则、IP 封锁、系统缓解措施、攻击模式等：https://vercel.com/changelog/manage-vercel-firewall-in-the-cli

items.original_text: i appreciate how seriously the team always takes these reports (even when the answer turns out to be 'i got used to the current level of magic and now i'd like more please') Tibo: Codex team is aware of reports of GPT-5.5 performing worse for some users and investigating. We don't have anything conclusive yet and systems are healthy but we will share updates as we go.

items.original_text: RT Thang Luong Very excited to share a new milestone in AI for Math: Aletheia, powered by Gemini Deep Think, was just used to autonomously solve a Kirby problem! “Kirby’s list” is a “compendium of the most important unsolved problems in topology, the study of deformable shapes” (Quanta magazine). 🧵
items.zh_text: 转发 Thang Luong：非常兴奋地分享人工智能在数学领域的一个新里程碑：由 Gemini Deep Think 驱动的 Aletheia 刚刚被用于自主解决一个 Kirby 问题！“Kirby 列表”是“拓扑学（可变形形状的研究）中最重要的未解决问题汇编”（Quanta 杂志）。🧵

items.original_text: RT David Weston (DWIZZZLE) http://cybergym.io just updated its leaderboard and MDASH is now #1 using a new multi-model approach. Huge credit to Taesoo Kim and the Autonomous Code Security team for pushing the frontier on AI-driven vulnerability discovery and defense https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/12/defense-at-ai-speed-microsofts-new-multi-model-agentic-security-system-tops-leading-industry-benchmark/
items.zh_text: Satya Nadella 转发了 David Weston (DWIZZZLE) 的推文：http://cybergym.io 刚刚更新了其排行榜，MDASH 凭借一种新的多模型方法跃居第一。非常感谢 Taesoo Kim 和自主代码安全团队在推动人工智能驱动的漏洞发现与防御前沿方面所做的贡献。https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/12/defense-at-ai-speed-microsofts-new-multi-model-agentic-security-system-tops-leading-industry-benchmark/

items.original_text: Our new multi-model agentic security system brings together more than 100 specialized agents across frontier and custom models to find exploitable bugs, delivering top performance on the CyberGym benchmark. We used it ahead of Patch Tuesday to help find and fix 16 vulnerabilities. Today we’re announcing that customers can sign up to test it in private preview. https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/12/defense-at-ai-speed-microsofts-new-multi-model-agentic-security-system-tops-leading-industry-benchmark/?v=1
items.zh_text: 我们的新型多模型自主安全系统整合了前沿模型与定制模型中的100多个专业智能体，用于发现可利用漏洞，在CyberGym基准测试中取得了顶尖性能。我们在补丁星期二之前使用该系统帮助发现并修复了16个漏洞。今天，我们宣布客户可以注册参与私人预览测试。

items.original_text: PayPal runs 74,000 weekly tasks in Perplexity Enterprise. Teams use it for model validation, channel performance, market trend research, competitive intelligence, and product analysis. Read the customer story: https://www.perplexity.ai/enterprise/customers/paypal
items.zh_text: PayPal 每周在 Perplexity Enterprise 上运行 74,000 个任务。团队将其用于模型验证、渠道绩效、市场趋势研究、竞争情报和产品分析。阅读客户案例：https://www.perplexity.ai/enterprise/customers/paypal

items.original_text: RT Alex Zhao parameter golf was a blast. 2,000+ submissions. 1,000+ verified github accounts. ideas ranging from quantization and depth recurrence to TTT LoRA, SSMs, H-nets, JEPA, and more. autoresearch made iteration dramatically faster — and led to emergent bulletin boards, issue threads, unofficial leaderboards, and agent-built writeups that helped everyone learn from everyone else. it felt like a glimpse of where interaction with AI is headed: humans setting taste and direction, agents helping explore, coordinate, and share what works. our goal was simple: make ml research accessible to anyone, anywhere. it was amazing to see that happen. full recap: https://openai.com/index/what-parameter-golf-taught-us/ future events: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/openai/form/open-ai-challenge-parameter-golf
items.zh_text: RT Alex Zhao 参数高尔夫太棒了。超过2000份提交，1000多个经过验证的GitHub账户。想法涵盖从量化、深度递归到TTT LoRA、SSMs、H-nets、JEPA等。自动研究让迭代速度大幅提升——并催生了公告板、问题讨论串、非官方排行榜以及由智能体撰写的总结，帮助大家互相学习。这让人一窥与AI互动的未来方向：人类设定品味和方向，智能体协助探索、协调并分享有效的方法。我们的目标很简单：让机器学习研究对任何地方的任何人都可及。看到这一切成真，真是太棒了。完整回顾：https://openai.com/index/what-parameter-golf-taught-us/ 未来活动：https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/openai/form/open-ai-challenge-parameter-golf

items.original_text: Really cool work from the team reimagining the mouse pointer to be intelligent! Try the prototype in @GoogleAIStudio it's pretty magical. Google DeepMind: We’re reimagining a 50-year-old interface - the mouse pointer - with AI. 🖱️ These experimental demos show how people can intuitively direct Gemini on their screens using motion, speech, and natural shorthand to get things done 🧵
items.zh_text: 来自团队的非常酷的工作，重新构想鼠标指针使其智能化！在@GoogleAIStudio中尝试原型，它相当神奇。Google DeepMind：我们正在用AI重新构想一个已有50年历史的界面——鼠标指针。🖱️这些实验性演示展示了人们如何通过动作、语音和自然简写直观地引导屏幕上的Gemini完成任务🧵

items.original_text: This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the generated file in your browser. I've also had some success asking the LLM to present its output as slideshows, etc. More generally, imo audio is the human-preferred input to AIs but vision (images/animations/video) is the preferred output from them. Around a ~third of our brains are a massively parallel processor dedicated to vision, it is the 10-lane superhighway of information into brain. As AI improves, I think we'll see a progression that takes advantage: 1) raw text (hard/effortful to read) 2) markdown (bold, italic, headings, tables, a bit easier on the eyes) <-- current default 3) HTML (still procedural with underlying code, but a lot more flexibility on the graphics, layout, even interactivity) <-- early but forming new good default ...4,5,6,... n) interactive neural videos/simulations Imo the extrapolation (though the technology doesn't exist just yet) ends in some kind of interactive videos generated directly by a diffusion neural net. Many open questions as to how exact/procedural "Software 1.0" artifacts (e.g. interactive simulations) may be woven together with neural artifacts (diffusion grids), but generally something in the direction of the recently viral https://x.com/zan2434/status/2046982383430496444 There are also improvements necessary and pending at the input. Audio nor text nor video alone are not enough, e.g. I feel a need to point/gesture to things on the screen, similar to all the things you would do with a person physically next to you and your computer screen. TLDR The input/output mind meld between humans and AIs is ongoing and there is a lot of work to do and significant progress to be made, way before jumping all the way into neuralink-esque BCIs and all that. For what's worth exploring at the current stage, hot tip try ask for HTML. Thariq: http://x.com/i/article/2052796100608974848
items.zh_text: 顺便说一句，这个方法效果很好：在提问结束时，要求你的大语言模型“以 HTML 格式组织回答”，然后在浏览器中查看生成的文件。我还曾成功让大语言模型以幻灯片等形式呈现输出。更广泛地说，我认为音频是人类偏好的 AI 输入方式，而视觉（图像/动画/视频）则是偏好的输出方式。我们大脑中大约三分之一的部分是专门用于视觉的大规模并行处理器，它是信息进入大脑的 10 车道超级高速公路。随着 AI 的进步，我认为我们会看到一系列利用这一优势的演进：1) 纯文本（阅读起来费力/费劲）2) Markdown（粗体、斜体、标题、表格，对眼睛稍微友好一些）<-- 当前默认 3) HTML（仍然有底层代码的过程性，但在图形、布局甚至交互性上灵活得多）<-- 早期但正在形成新的良好默认值 ...4,5,6,... n) 交互式神经视频/模拟。我认为外推（尽管技术尚未存在）最终会走向由扩散神经网络直接生成的某种交互式视频。关于如何将精确/过程性的“软件 1.0”产物（例如交互式模拟）与神经产物（扩散网格）编织在一起，还有许多悬而未决的问题，但总体方向类似于最近爆火的 https://x.com/zan2434/status/2046982383430496444。在输入方面也有必要且待进行的改进。仅靠音频、文本或视频是不够的，例如，我觉得需要指向/手势屏幕上的内容，类似于你与一个物理上在你旁边的人以及你的电脑屏幕所做的一切。简而言之，人类与 AI 之间的输入/输出思维融合正在进行中，还有很多工作要做，并且需要取得重大进展，远在直接跳到 Neuralink 式的脑机接口等之前。就当前阶段值得探索的内容而言，一个小窍门是尝试要求 HTML。Thariq: http://x.com/i/article/2052796100608974848
items.media_note: 该动态可能包含 X 原生媒体，当前 RSS 未提供视频文件，请打开原帖观看。

items.original_text: Excel has quietly been Turing complete for a long time. Nice to see it now edging toward "AI complete"—SGD, attention, next-token prediction… all in cells. Austin Henley: Excel Copilot one-shotted a tiny GPT-style language model for me inside a spreadsheet: embeddings, causal attention, weights trained with stochastic gradient descent, next-token prediction, and a live slider to watch it learn.
items.zh_text: Excel 长期以来一直默默图灵完备。很高兴看到它现在正迈向“AI 完备”——随机梯度下降、注意力机制、下一个词元预测……全都在单元格里。Austin Henley：Excel Copilot 在电子表格中为我一次性构建了一个微型 GPT 风格语言模型：嵌入层、因果注意力、通过随机梯度下降训练的权重、下一个词元预测，以及一个实时滑块来观察学习过程。

items.original_text: This is the the quote I've been citing a lot recently. kache: you can outsource your thinking but you cannot outsource your understanding
items.zh_text: 这是我最近经常引用的一句话。kache：你可以外包你的思考，但你不能外包你的理解。

items.original_text: Fireside chat at Sequoia Ascent 2026 from a ~week ago. Some highlights: The first theme I tried to push on is that LLMs are about a lot more than just speeding up what existed before (e.g. coding). Three examples of new horizons: 1. menugen: an app that can be fully engulfed by LLMs, with no classical code needed: input an image, output an image and an LLM can natively do the thing. 2. install .md skills instead of install .sh scripts. Why create a complex Software 1.0 bash script for e.g. installing a piece of software if you can write the installation out in words and say "just show this to your LLM". The LLM is an advanced interpreter of English and can intelligently target installation to your setup, debug everything inline, etc. 3. LLM knowledge bases as an example of something that was *impossible* with classical code because it's computation over unstructured data (knowledge) from arbitrary sources and in arbitrary formats, including simply text articles etc. I pushed on these because in every new paradigm change, the obvious things are always in the realm of speeding up or somehow improving what existed, but here we have examples of functionality that either suddenly perhaps shouldn't even exist (1,2), or was fundamentally not possible before (3). The second (ongoing) theme is trying to explain the pattern of jaggedness in LLMs. How it can be true that a single artifact will simultaneously 1) coherently refactor a 100,000-line code base *and* 2) tell you to walk to the car wash to wash your car. I previously wrote about the source of this as having to do with verifiability of a domain, here I expand on this as having to also do with economics because revenue/TAM dictates what the frontier labs choose to package into training data distributions during RL. You're either in the data distribution (on the rails of the RL circuits) and flying or you're off-roading in the jungle with a machete, in relative terms. Still not 100% satisfied with this, but it's an ongoing struggle to build an accurate model of LLM capabilities if you wish to practically take advantage of their power while avoiding their pitfalls, which brings me to... Last theme is the agent-native economy. The decomposition of products and services into sensors, actuators and logic (split up across all of 1.0/2.0/3.0 computing paradigms), how we can make information maximally legible to LLMs, some words on the quickly emerging agentic engineering and its skill set, related hiring practices, etc., possibly even hints/dreams of fully neural computing handling the vast majority of computation with some help from (classical) CPU coprocessors. Stephanie Zhan: @karpathy and I are back! At @sequoia AI Ascent 2026. And a lot has changed. Last year, he coined “vibe coding”. This year, he’s never felt more behind as a programmer. The big shift: vibe coding raised the floor. Agentic engineering raises the ceiling. We talk about what it
items.zh_text: 大约一周前在红杉资本2026年峰会上的一场炉边谈话。一些亮点：我试图强调的第一个主题是，LLM远不止是加速现有事物（例如编程）。三个新视野的例子：1. menugen：一个可以完全被LLM吞噬的应用，无需任何经典代码：输入一张图像，输出一张图像，LLM可以原生地完成这件事。2. 安装.md技能而非安装.sh脚本。为什么要为安装一个软件而创建一个复杂的Software 1.0 bash脚本，如果你可以用文字写出安装步骤并说“只需把这个给你的LLM看”？LLM是一个高级的英语解释器，可以智能地针对你的设置进行安装，内联调试所有内容等。3. LLM知识库作为一个经典代码*不可能*实现的例子，因为它是对来自任意来源和任意格式的非结构化数据（知识）的计算，包括简单的文本文章等。我强调这些是因为在每个新的范式变革中，显而易见的事情总是属于加速或以某种方式改进现有事物的范畴，但在这里我们有功能的例子，这些功能要么突然可能不应该存在（1、2），要么以前根本不可能实现（3）。第二个（持续的）主题是试图解释LLM中的锯齿状模式。为什么一个单一工件会同时1）连贯地重构一个10万行的代码库*并且*2）告诉你步行去洗车场洗你的车。我之前写过关于这个来源的文章，认为它与领域的可验证性有关，这里我将其扩展为也与经济学有关，因为收入/TAM决定了前沿实验室在强化学习期间选择将什么打包到训练数据分布中。你要么在数据分布中（在强化学习电路的轨道上）飞行，要么相对而言，在丛林中用砍刀越野。对此我仍然不是100%满意，但如果你希望实际利用LLM的力量同时避免其陷阱，这是一个持续的斗争，以建立一个准确的LLM能力模型，这引出了……最后一个主题是代理原生经济。将产品和服务分解为传感器、执行器和逻辑（分布在所有1.0/2.0/3.0计算范式中），我们如何使信息对LLM最大程度地可读，一些关于快速兴起的代理工程及其技能集的讨论，相关的招聘实践等，甚至可能暗示/梦想完全神经计算在（经典）CPU协处理器的一些帮助下处理绝大多数计算。Stephanie Zhan：@karpathy 和我回来了！在 @sequoia AI Ascent 2026。很多事情都变了。去年，他创造了“氛围编码”这个词。今年，他作为程序员从未感到如此落后。巨大的转变：氛围编码提高了下限。代理工程提高了上限。我们谈论这意味着什么。

items.original_text: RT Dan Scavino Jr.🇺🇸🦅 On behalf of Erin and me, thank you to everyone for the love and support we've received. We both had an unforgettable wedding day, and we'll never forget it. We're humbled and grateful to all. THANK YOU! 🙏🏻🇺🇸🦅🚀
items.zh_text: 转发 Dan Scavino Jr.🇺🇸🦅 代表 Erin 和我，感谢大家给予我们的爱与支持。我们度过了一个难忘的婚礼日，永远不会忘记。我们谦卑而感激所有人。谢谢你们！🙏🏻🇺🇸🦅🚀

items.original_text: RT Fox News RECORD BREAKER: “Melania,” a documentary following the first lady in the 20 days before President Trump’s second inauguration, earns $8 million opening weekend — reportedly making it the biggest documentary debut in more than a decade.
items.zh_text: 转发福克斯新闻 破纪录者：《梅拉尼娅》——一部记录特朗普总统第二次就职前20天第一夫人生活的纪录片，首周末票房收入800万美元——据报道，这使其成为十多年来最大的纪录片首映。
